title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Pre-pandemic Influenza Vaccine Framework Publication date: 27 February 2026 The Pre-pandemic Influenza Vaccine Framework provides guidance for use of the pre-pandemic influenza vaccine. This vaccine supports New Zealand’s initial response to an avian influenza health emergency or pandemic. Publication Influenza Pandemics Emergency management Public health

## Pre-pandemic Influenza Vaccine Framework
The Pre-pandemic Influenza Vaccine Framework provides guidance for use of the pre-pandemic influenza vaccine. This vaccine supports New Zealand’s initial response to an avian influenza health emergency or pandemic.
- Emergency management
- Migraine 2023/24: New Zealand Health Survey Publication date: 22 January 2026 This page presents information collected from adults aged 15 years and over in the migraine module of the 2023/24 New Zealand Health Survey. Publication Statistics Surveys

## Migraine 2023/24: New Zealand Health Survey
This page presents information collected from adults aged 15 years and over in the migraine module of the 2023/24 New Zealand Health Survey.
- Meeting sleep guidelines 2024/25: New Zealand Health Survey Publication date: 22 January 2026 The New Zealand Health Survey asks respondents how much sleep they usually get in a 24-hour period. This page summarises sleep results, focusing on how different population groups compare when it comes to meeting the sleep duration recommendations. Publication Statistics Surveys

## Meeting sleep guidelines 2024/25: New Zealand Health Survey
The New Zealand Health Survey asks respondents how much sleep they usually get in a 24-hour period. This page summarises sleep results, focusing on how different population groups compare when it comes to meeting the sleep duration recommendations.
- Rural Health Roadshow Insights Report Publication date: 22 December 2025 The Rural Health Roadshow Insights Report captures findings from visits to 13 rural locations throughout 2025, reviewing progress against the Rural Health Strategy. The roadshow provided was a valuable opportunity to engage with rural communities, recognise successes, and identify areas for improvement. Publication Rural health

## Rural Health Roadshow Insights Report
The Rural Health Roadshow Insights Report captures findings from visits to 13 rural locations throughout 2025, reviewing progress against the Rural Health Strategy. The roadshow provided was a valuable opportunity to engage with rural communities, recognise successes, and identify areas for improvement.
- Economic and Social Costing of Gambling in Aotearoa New Zealand 2023–24 Publication date: 19 December 2025 Taking a public health approach, this study examines the economic and social costs associated with gambling harm in New Zealand. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research

## Economic and Social Costing of Gambling in Aotearoa New Zealand 2023–24
Taking a public health approach, this study examines the economic and social costs associated with gambling harm in New Zealand.
- Development and Validation of the Gambling Help Seeking Assessment Tool Publication date: 19 December 2025 This research examines how gambling help-seeking is defined, measured and enacted across a wide range if behaviours and supports. It captures how people experiencing gambling harm and professionals conceptualise help-seeking. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research

## Development and Validation of the Gambling Help Seeking Assessment Tool
This research examines how gambling help-seeking is defined, measured and enacted across a wide range if behaviours and supports. It captures how people experiencing gambling harm and professionals conceptualise help-seeking.
- Complementary and Alternative Interventions to Support Recovery from Gambling Harm Publication date: 19 December 2025 This study profiles the health and wellbeing of people experiencing gambling harm in New Zealand and presents findings of research into evidence-based complementary and alternative interventions that may be effective to improve health and wellbeing. The report also provides suggestions for how these interventions could be implemented. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research

## Complementary and Alternative Interventions to Support Recovery from Gambling Harm
This study profiles the health and wellbeing of people experiencing gambling harm in New Zealand and presents findings of research into evidence-based complementary and alternative interventions that may be effective to improve health and wellbeing. The report also provides suggestions for how these interventions could be implemented.
- A Qualitative Study Exploring the Gambling Experiences of Older Adults in New Zealand Publication date: 19 December 2025 This research explores the gambling behaviours, preferences and perceptions of older adults (aged 50+) living in New Zealand, with the aim of understanding older adults’ attitudes towards gambling. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research Older people (65+)

## A Qualitative Study Exploring the Gambling Experiences of Older Adults in New Zealand
This research explores the gambling behaviours, preferences and perceptions of older adults (aged 50+) living in New Zealand, with the aim of understanding older adults’ attitudes towards gambling.
- Factors Influencing Gambling Harm for the Asian Population in New Zealand: An empirical study to test the conceptual Asian Integrated Tree Model Publication date: 19 December 2025 This research aimed to empirically test factors relating to New Zealand Asian migrants’ experiences with gambling and mental health, as identified in the Integrated Tree Model, supporting its use as a holistic approach to addressing gambling harm. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research Asian health

## Factors Influencing Gambling Harm for the Asian Population in New Zealand: An empirical study to test the conceptual Asian Integrated Tree Model
This research aimed to empirically test factors relating to New Zealand Asian migrants’ experiences with gambling and mental health, as identified in the Integrated Tree Model, supporting its use as a holistic approach to addressing gambling harm.
- Voices of Lived Experience: Asian people’s journeys from gambling harm to recovery Publication date: 19 December 2025 This research examines the recovery experiences of Asian individuals living in New Zealand with lived experience of gambling harm. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research Asian health

## Voices of Lived Experience: Asian people’s journeys from gambling harm to recovery
This research examines the recovery experiences of Asian individuals living in New Zealand with lived experience of gambling harm.
- The Convergence of Simulated Gambling, Monetary Gambling and Gambling Harm: An integrative literature review of stimulus and suppression pathways Publication date: 19 December 2025 This research presents a literature review on the convergence of simulated gambling, monetary gambling and gambling harm. It also reviews evidence for risk and protective factors for gambling harm that might arise from simulated gambling and suggests strategies to help reduce this harm. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research

## The Convergence of Simulated Gambling, Monetary Gambling and Gambling Harm: An integrative literature review of stimulus and suppression pathways
This research presents a literature review on the convergence of simulated gambling, monetary gambling and gambling harm. It also reviews evidence for risk and protective factors for gambling harm that might arise from simulated gambling and suggests strategies to help reduce this harm.
- Understanding the Reasons, Impact, and Support Needs of Older People Experiencing Gambling Harm: Research report Publication date: 19 December 2025 This research investigates the reasons, impact, and support needs of older people experiencing gambling harm in New Zealand. It includes a global desktop review and in-depth interviews with 29 older gamblers, 27 families, 19 gambling harm service providers, and three gambling venue workers. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research

## Understanding the Reasons, Impact, and Support Needs of Older People Experiencing Gambling Harm: Research report
This research investigates the reasons, impact, and support needs of older people experiencing gambling harm in New Zealand. It includes a global desktop review and in-depth interviews with 29 older gamblers, 27 families, 19 gambling harm service providers, and three gambling venue workers.
- Pulling Out of Gambling Treatment: Research, service, practitioner, and client perspectives Publication date: 19 December 2025 This research explores definitions, estimates, predictors, reasons, consequences, and potential solutions related to pulling out of formal psychological gambling treatment. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research

## Pulling Out of Gambling Treatment: Research, service, practitioner, and client perspectives
This research explores definitions, estimates, predictors, reasons, consequences, and potential solutions related to pulling out of formal psychological gambling treatment.
- Should I Stay or Should I Go Now? Systemic mapping of barriers to generate solutions for equitable access to gambling harm services Publication date: 19 December 2025 This research explores issues around equitable access to gambling harm services, recognising that access to care is shaped by a combination of institutional, social, and cultural forces. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research

## Should I Stay or Should I Go Now? Systemic mapping of barriers to generate solutions for equitable access to gambling harm services
This research explores issues around equitable access to gambling harm services, recognising that access to care is shaped by a combination of institutional, social, and cultural forces.
- Understanding Gambling Relapse and Associated Factors: A longitudinal approach Publication date: 19 December 2025 This research sought to understand what constitutes gambling relapse and to identify factors that are associated with relapse. Publication Gambling harm Gambling research

## Understanding Gambling Relapse and Associated Factors: A longitudinal approach
This research sought to understand what constitutes gambling relapse and to identify factors that are associated with relapse.
